summary: Supporting cusps play a crucial role in stabilizing the dentition during lateral movements by engaging with the central fosses of opposing teeth. This alignment occurs through direct occlusal contacts such as the maxillary buccal and mandibular lingual cusps contacting the central fossa areas during excursive motion.
